qas.a:
___________________________ - determines the most effective and efficient way to ensure training standards and skill level requirements for a career field are accurate and meet the needs of the Air Force

- Main goal of the [same answer]_________ is to establish a viable CFETP

- Typically initiated every other year, but can occur at any time to deal with changes, deficiencies, or other impacts on training

1C8X3 _________________ oversees the career fields and ensures radar and airfield systems operate and support mission goals

Advisor for all matters affecting the RAWS career field

Communicates directly with MFM and Air Education and Training Command (AETC) training managers to disseminate all Air Force and career field policies and program requirements

Serves as the chairperson for the Specialty Training Requirements Team (STRT) and Utilization and Training Workshops (U&TW)

Advises MAJCOM commanders and their staff on manpower utilization and training issues

Assist with the dissemination of information regarding Air Force and career field policies, plans, programs, and procedures to subordinate units

Normally assigned to the ranking 1C8X3 superintendent or chief enlisted manager (CEM) on-station

Manages system analysis and design, programming, systems operation, maintenance, resource management and security management

1C8 TRAINING DEVELOPMENT
_______________________________________ - meeting that includes the AFCFM, MFMs, CDC writers, Qualification Training Flight (Q-flight), and subject matter experts (SME)

- CFETP is written and updated, to include what will be trained at the tech training school house and what topics are covered within the CDCs

1C8 TRAINING DEVELOPMENT
__________________ - chartered by the AFFSA Headquarters Commander to facilitate the implementation of lead command processes

- Meets annually to addresses multi-command issues, and ensure the implementation and sustainment of multi-command systems and equipment

- MAJCOM requirements for new common and command-unique equipment for the NAS and deployable systems to support current and future combat operations

- System modernization and sustainment efforts

1C8 TRAINING DEVELOPMENT
______________ - convenes annually to address training management issues affecting AFSCs

- Review training concerns and successes and coordinate training initiatives

1C8 TRAINING DEVELOPMENT
______________ - meeting headed up by AFFSA for RAWS and includes a complete review of the systems program to include modifications, funding, safety concerns, supply issues, etc.

Performs Radar, Airfield & Weather Systems functions

Installs, removes, and relocates radar, airfield and weather systems

Repairs, overhauls, and modifies radar, airfield and weather systems

Develops methods for improving maintenance

Establish requirements

Deploy

HEADQUARTERS AIR FORCE (HAF)
______________________ - will perform AF-level career field management functions for Ground Radar and Airfield Systems

qas.q: HQ AIR FORCE FLIGHT STANDARDS AGENCY (AFFSA)
qas.a:
________________ is a Field Operating Agency organized under the Deputy Chief of Staff, Operations, Plans and Requirements, Headquarters, Air Force.

______________'s mission is to increase the maximum effectiveness of USAF global air operations.

Provide status on training, manning, time compliance technical orders, upcoming modifications, installs, or other projects

Enforce sound maintenance, supply discipline, and financial management practices

Authorize use, approval and processing of local maintenance quality control checklists

Review evaluation reports that return with an overall unsatisfactory

All other evaluations reviewed at the _______________'s discretion

MAINTENANCE WORK CENTER (MWC)/PRODUCTION WORK CENTER (PWC) RESPONSIBILITIES

Planning/scheduling production, ordering/managing materials, and documenting/tracking maintenance actions for all base ____________ and __________________

Provide 24-hour POC to all customers, command post, and up-channels to address maintenance needs.

Document, maintain, and report system and/or equipment status and coordinate status changes with all concerned agencies and include start time and estimated time of return to operation

qas.q: Job Control Number (JCN), Integrated Maintenance Data System (IMDS), Regional Maintenance Center (RMC)
qas.a:
MAINTENANCE WORK CENTER (MWC)/PRODUCTION WORK CENTER (PWC) RESPONSIBILITIES

Assign a ____________________ when a loss of capability or function in either equipment/system or service occurs and capture customer service request tracking number in the _________________________ job subject line/narrative

This will ensure proper tracking of outages and performance issues

Preventive maintenance inspections, routine maintenance and any maintenance requested by the _________________________.

qas.q: depot, maintenance assistance requests, Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)
qas.a:
MAINTENANCE WORK CENTER (MWC)/PRODUCTION WORK CENTER (PWC) RESPONSIBILITIES

Act as the base POC for _______ and _________________________ .

Review and accomplish all applicable TCTOs, Time Change Items and document in IMDS

Since many Ground Radar and Airfield Systems utilize communication equipment to function properly, the MWC/PWC will:

Ensure a ________________________ is established between the OSS and the Communication Squadron (CS) to identify restoral priorities and all mission critical systems and circuits

qas.q: Airfield Systems MWC/PWC, RMC, an aircraft mishap
qas.a:
AIRFIELD OPERATIONS FLIGHT COMMANDER

Ensure base agencies coordinate digging permits in the vicinity of ATCALS through _________________ for review and approval

If the local MWC/PWC have any questions or concerns about a digging permit the _______ can be contacted for guidance

Failure to do so can lead to the damage and/or disabling of an ATCAL, which could lead to ______________

qas.q: MWC/PWC supervisor, maintenance functions, personnel assignment
qas.a:
SUPERINTENDENT/CHIEF OF MAINTENANCE RESPONSIBILITIES

If there is no superintendent, the ____________________ will accomplish these duties

Advise and assist unit leaders and technicians in managing and administering maintenance programs.

Make frequent visits to all ______________________ to ensure they are familiar with all maintenance activities

Control ___________________ within the maintenance activity and advise leaders on manning levels

MWC/PWC SUPERVISOR RESPONSIBILITIES

Ensure personnel have a thorough knowledge of their duties and comply with ________________, ___________________ and ______________

qas.q: technical data, support items, up-to-date status/information
qas.a:
MWC/PWC SUPERVISOR RESPONSIBILITIES

Ensure upgrade training and maintenance OJT training programs are established and effectively managed

Schedule, monitor, control, and coordinate efforts for production

Dispatch personnel with the _____________ and _______________ needed to troubleshoot, repair, and restore systems in an expedient manner

Reviews all open jobs for accuracy and to ensure the most __________________________ is captured

Review supply support requirements in any OPLAN, Programming Plan (PPLAN) and Program Action Directives (PAD) that task the work centers

Ensure written guidance exists to monitor and control shop/operating stocks, which should include the consumption of work order residue prior to using bench stock

Coordinate with support agencies and assist work center personnel by expediting all supply transactions

Primary liaison between the work center and LRS and ensures all required parts, TCTO kits and supplies are requested and delivered

May be completed as an additional duty by work center personnel if the unit does not have a designated liaison

______________ and ____________ are responsible to provide written procedures to ensure the LRS Liaison has the responsibility and authority to coordinate between the work center and LRS/supply activities

Deals directly with ATC and the base MWC to address system monitoring and repair requirements

TO-00-20-10 establishes procedures for the Local Work Center (LWC) to support the RMC and provides required responsibilities for all MWC/PWCs that work on RMC equipment

qas.q: 1. CONUS theaters, 2. European, 3. Pacific, HQ AFFSA/XMR
qas.a:
REGIONAL MAINTENANCE CENTER

ATCALS support includes three fixed-base RMCs in the 1. ____________ , 2. ______________ , and 3. ______________.

__________________ manages all three RMCs

1. ____________ RMC (HQ AFFSA/XMRC) located at Oklahoma City, services the continental U.S. as well as Greenland, and South/Central America

2. ____________ RMC (HQ AFFSA/XMRE) is located at Kapaun AB, Germany and will service sites in Europe and Africa

3. _____________ RMC (HQ AFFSA/XMRP) is located at Yokota AB, Japan, and will service sites in the Pacific, including Alaska & Hawaii

qas.q: AF Engineering and Technical Service (AFETS), Special Maintenance Team (SMT), ATCALS Radar Evaluation, 24, 7
qas.a:
REGIONAL MAINTENANCE CENTER

Maintains and monitors equipment within their respective region, but can be called upon to support other regions

Provide all traditional ______________________, __________________ , and _______________________ support

Perform routine and specialized maintenance for remotely maintained equipment to include resets, alignments, adjustments and flight check inspections

- Respond to troubleshoot failures and maintenance alerts ___ hours per day, ___ days per week

qas.q: 30, 60, first in, first out, outage criticality, maintenance data terminal (MDT)
qas.a:
REGIONAL MAINTENANCE CENTER

Expected response time is to be no more than ____ minutes during normal duty hours, ___ minutes for non-duty hours

System outage responses will be ________, _______.

System restoral will be based on ________________, i.e. red outage vs. amber outage

NAVAIDs are controlled and monitored from the RMC with a PC equipped with _____________________ software.

- Technicians in the RMC use the MDT for routine status monitoring and maintenance

qas.q: base civil engineering (BCE), Local maintenance personnel
qas.a:
REGIONAL MAINTENANCE CENTER

Request the local maintenance work center to open jobs with ____________________________ anytime an ATCAL experiences an issue such as a local power outage, environmental control unit failure (i.e. Air Conditioner), generator maintenance requirement, or telephone line/circuit outage

Orders parts for RM equipment and notify the local maintenance unit of the expected delivery date

_____________________________ will conduct the parts replacement under the direction of RM personnel

- RM personnel will respond on-site as soon as possible or in conjunction with required parts arriving on site

REGIONAL MAINTENANCE CENTER

Contacts the appropriate ATC organization following restoral and return system control and request ___________________________ through the base Air Traffic Manager, AOF/CC, or OSS/OSA

__________________ assist with site visits, conduct alignments and repairs, and modifications of RAWS equipment

MAJCOM FUNCTIONAL MANAGER (MFM)

Deals with _____________ , _____________________ and __________________ on the needs within the command

Serves as command focal point to implement Air Force guidance and directives concerning systems modifications, programmed and Mobile Depot Maintenance (MDM), TO improvements, Source, Maintenance, and Recoverability (SMR) code change requests, deficiency reports, product improvement reports, MICT checklists, AF Maintenance Special Instructions (AFMSIs), corrosion control, maintenance management directives, and T.O. 00-series publications

Identifies a central point of contact for administrative issues, Critical Outage Reports, and the Health of ATCALS

MAJCOM FUNCTIONAL MANAGER (MFM)

Designates which __________________________ requires the work centers to maintain a permanent history on

Central waiver authority for Tier ____ and ____ compliance items/functions

Key focal point for recommended changes to AFI13-204v4 and ____________

MAJCOM FUNCTIONAL MANAGER (MFM)

Reviews and oversees Compliance with _____________________ in accordance with AFI 90-201

Provide guidance to the work centers on the application of the MICT checklists

Works with the MAJCOM IG to establish inspection schedules for the units

May accompany the IG team as a SME
CMSgt assigned to Headquarters Air Force (HAF) and is responsible for organizing and managing one or more enlisted career fields

Establishes requirements for entry into the career field, managing skill-level training requirements, and developing and managing career-long training plan requirements and programs

Construct viable career paths, evaluate training effectiveness, monitor the health and manning of the career field, and provide input on manning personnel policies and programs

Implement and advise Air Staff on changes to force management policies and programs, develop contingency planning actions, validate deployment requirements, and verify workforce availability

Advised on all matters affecting the career field

CAREER FIELD MANAGER (CFM)

Ensure their career fields are responsive to both _________ and ___________ needs of the AF

qas.a:

___________________________________ appoints the RAWS CFM

Possesses final authority to waive _____________ requirements, which include _________________

CAREER FIELD MANAGER (CFM)

Assist _________________ and ________________ with planning, developing, implementing, and maintaining all AFSC-specific training courses

Assists in the development of AFSC-related manpower standards

Ranking 1C8 superintendent or chief enlisted manager (CEM) on station

Responsible for manning and staffing personnel within their AFSC and allocating them fairly between organizations on base as applicable

Manages system analysis and design, programming, systems operation, maintenance, resource management, and security management

BASE FUNCTIONAL MANAGER (BFM)

Liaison between the organizations on their ______________ and the _______________

BASE FUNCTIONAL MANAGER (BFM)
